introSection: Soarin' Eagle is a steel flying roller coaster located at Luna Park in New York, New York, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content: Soarin' Eagle originally opened at Six Flags Elitch Gardens as Flying Coaster in Denver, Colorado, USA on May 18, 2002. It was installed by Martin & Vleminckx. It is the prototype Volare from Zamperla and suffered many operational problems at Elitch Gardens. It was closed in 2007 but remained standing but not operating until 2010, when it was dismantled and was sold back to Zamperla, being relocated to Luna Park, where it was repainted and opened in April 2011 as Soarin' Eagle.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * Ride page on the park's website

fullText:
Soarin' Eagle is a steel flying roller coaster located at Luna Park in New York, New York, USA.

Soarin' Eagle originally opened at Six Flags Elitch Gardens as Flying Coaster in Denver, Colorado, USA on May 18, 2002. It was installed by Martin & Vleminckx. It is the prototype Volare from Zamperla and suffered many operational problems at Elitch Gardens. It was closed in 2007 but remained standing but not operating until 2010, when it was dismantled and was sold back to Zamperla, being relocated to Luna Park, where it was repainted and opened in April 2011 as Soarin' Eagle.




 * Ride page on the park's website
